methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $33M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QunaSys is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Tennin Yan that has raised $33M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable firms: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and others.

Stage-progression methodology, the sole applicable triangulation approach, was employed given insufficient depth in same-stage or adjacent-stage peer comparables within quantum computing. Using total capital raised ($33M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement yields $63M. This method produced a median valuation estimate. A low-end scenario of $53M and high-end scenario of $72M were derived by applying ±15% sensitivity bounds around the stage-progression result.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $72M, with $63M as the central estimate. Primary uncertainties include the absence of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peer data to cross-validate the stage-progression multiple, the reliance on total capital raised as a valuation proxy rather than disclosed round pricing, and quantum computing's nascent stage, which limits historical precedent for progression multiples. Any valuation conclusion should be treated as preliminary pending access to broader comparables or additional company-specific operational metrics.
